  "findings": [
    "Condensate line is missing a trap or is sloped incorrectly, preventing proper drainage of acidic condensate."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a proper condensate trap as specified by the manufacturer.",
    "Re-slope the condensate line to maintain a minimum 1/4 inch per foot downward slope toward the drain."
  ],
  "rationale": "A high-efficiency condensing furnace produces acidic condensate that must be drained via a trapped and properly sloped line. A missing trap can allow flue gases to escape into the living space, and improper slope will cause condensate to back up, potentially damaging the furnace or causing a safety shutdown.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54",
    "IRC Chapter 14 / Section M1411.3"
  ]
}
